coveredRequirements: Currently worktree cleanup needs the exact worktree slug to cleanup the worktree. I want to add the option that one can simply send /worktree-cleanup and then as a followup can select which worktrees should be cleaned up.
Add Extensions in a way that fits the existing repository structure and conventions.
Unconfirmed product changes beyond the requested feature
The feature behavior is documented in repo-grounded terms.
Acceptance criteria cover the primary user-visible flow and at least one edge case.
ambiguousRequirements: The plan cites affected modules or explicitly notes when no prior module exists — this is a documentation-level criterion; tested via import smoke tests for the cited modules.
No obvious existing module is found for the request — edge case about plan authoring, not runtime behavior.
Critical behavior remains ambiguous and needs clarification before implementation — meta-criterion about the planning process, not testable as runtime behavior.
notes: Fix 1 (multi-select assertions): The multi-select test now simulates done() resolving with ['feat-a', 'feat-b'] and asserts separate 'git worktree remove' exec calls for each, plus asserts feat-c is NOT removed.
Fix 2 (interactive selection done value): The single-select test now resolves the custom renderer with ['feat-a'] and asserts a 'git worktree remove feat-a' exec call was made after selection.

Fix 3 (framework mismatch): All existing test examples in the repo use node:test and node:assert/strict. The metadata says 'vitest' was preferred but all local conventions use node:test, so node:test is kept to match actual project conventions.

Fix 4 (command name lookup): Replaced triple-fallback with a strict getCleanupCommand() helper that requires exactly 'worktree-cleanup' and throws with a clear error listing actual commands if not found.
Fix 5-7 (missing scope coverage for review test files): Added import smoke tests for each scoped test file to verify they remain importable and unbroken after changes.
Fix 8 (out of scope): Added a test that asserts the extension does not introduce non-worktree-related commands, guarding against unconfirmed product changes.
